gaps.title: Subscription state machine race condition
gaps.breaks: Two webhook events processed simultaneously causing invalid state transitions
gaps.scenario: Payment fails and subscription_updated webhook arrives at same time, leaving subscription in inconsistent past_due/active state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent processing of payment_failed and subscription_updated webhooks for the same subscription. Verify that the final state is consistent and no race condition occurs. Use threading or asyncio to process events simultaneously.

gaps.title: Webhook replay attack vulnerability
gaps.breaks: Identical webhook reprocessed causing duplicate subscription actions
gaps.scenario: Attacker captures and resends subscription_created webhook, creating duplicate billing entries or subscription states
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies the PaddleHandler properly implements idempotency keys. Send identical subscription_created webhook twice and verify the second request is rejected or handled without side effects. Check for duplicate database entries.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation leak in webhook processing
gaps.breaks: One tenant's webhook events affect another tenant's data
gaps.scenario: Tenant A's subscription webhook is processed with incorrect tenant context, causing Tenant B's subscription to be modified
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in webhook processing. Send a webhook for Tenant A with modified headers or data that mimics Tenant B's ID. Verify that Tenant A's data remains unchanged and Tenant B's data is not affected.

gaps.title: Partial failure in subscription cancellation
gaps.breaks: Cancellation webhook partially processed leaving system in inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: subscription_canceled webhook updates subscription state but fails to update related billing records, causing continued billing for canceled subscription
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a failure during subscription cancellation processing. Verify that when the state update succeeds but a subsequent database operation fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and the subscription remains in its previous state.

gaps.title: Missing webhook event validation
gaps.breaks: Malformed or unexpected webhook events processed incorrectly
gaps.scenario: Paddle sends malformed subscription_updated event with missing required fields, causing application error or incorrect state transition
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies the PaddleHandler properly validates all webhook event payloads. Send malformed webhook events with missing, null, or invalid fields. Verify the system handles these gracefully without crashing or entering inconsistent states.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 5

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Subscription state machine race condition
What breaks: Two webhook events processed simultaneously causing invalid state transitions
Real scenario: Payment fails and subscription_updated webhook arrives at same time, leaving subscription in inconsistent past_due/active state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent processing of payment_failed and subscription_updated webhooks for the same subscription. Verify that the final state is consistent and no race condition occurs. Use threading or asyncio to process events simultaneously.

GAP 2
Severity: HIGH
Title: Webhook replay attack vulnerability
What breaks: Identical webhook reprocessed causing duplicate subscription actions
Real scenario: Attacker captures and resends subscription_created webhook, creating duplicate billing entries or subscription states
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies the PaddleHandler properly implements idempotency keys. Send identical subscription_created webhook twice and verify the second request is rejected or handled without side effects. Check for duplicate database entries.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation leak in webhook processing
What breaks: One tenant's webhook events affect another tenant's data
Real scenario: Tenant A's subscription webhook is processed with incorrect tenant context, causing Tenant B's subscription to be modified
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in webhook processing. Send a webhook for Tenant A with modified headers or data that mimics Tenant B's ID. Verify that Tenant A's data remains unchanged and Tenant B's data is not affected.

GAP 4
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Partial failure in subscription cancellation
What breaks: Cancellation webhook partially processed leaving system in inconsistent state
Real scenario: subscription_canceled webhook updates subscription state but fails to update related billing records, causing continued billing for canceled subscription
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a failure during subscription cancellation processing. Verify that when the state update succeeds but a subsequent database operation fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and the subscription remains in its previous state.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Missing webhook event validation
What breaks: Malformed or unexpected webhook events processed incorrectly
Real scenario: Paddle sends malformed subscription_updated event with missing required fields, causing application error or incorrect state transition
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies the PaddleHandler properly validates all webhook event payloads. Send malformed webhook events with missing, null, or invalid fields. Verify the system handles these gracefully without crashing or entering inconsistent states.
